prompt:
A researcher claims that library visits in the downtown branch increased steadily from 2020 to 2023.

Which finding from the table most directly supports the claim?

choices.text: The value changed by the same amount every year, a constant 13 thousands per year.
choices.rationale: The year-to-year changes vary, so the change was not a constant 13 thousands.

choices.text: The value rose each year, moving from 207 to 271 thousands over the period.
choices.rationale: Correct. This matches the steady, one-direction trend the claim describes across every year shown.

choices.text: The value rose by more than 35 thousands in a single year.
choices.rationale: The largest one-year change was only 20 thousands.

choices.text: Almost all of the change happened between 2020 and 2020, with little movement afterward.
choices.rationale: The value changes in every year shown, not just the first span.

explanation: The supporting statement must reflect the table's actual, consistent trend across all the years shown.

table.caption: Library visits in the downtown branch (thousands)
